title: Tiniaria × heterocarpa
intro: Tiniaria × heterocarpa, also known as Pacific Yarrow, is a hybrid perennial plant species of the daisy family, characterized by its fern-like leaves and tall, branching stems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, providing relief from colds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Tiniaria × heterocarpa include sesquiterpene lactones, volatile oils, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal ailments.
